name: Constant Contact API
what_it_does: Constant Contact REST API v3 — email marketing for small businesses with contact list management, email campaign creation, and event/survey tools, with OAuth 2.0 authentication for third-party integrations.
use_cases: Agents managing small business contact lists — syncing customer data from POS or CRM into Constant Contact
Campaign automation — agents scheduling email newsletters based on content calendar or event triggers
Event marketing — agents creating Constant Contact events and managing registrations for small business events
Contact segmentation — agents building targeted lists based on customer purchase history or behavior data
Integration sync — agents keeping Constant Contact contacts in sync with e-commerce platforms like Shopify
not_for: Advanced behavioral automation — Klaviyo, Drip, or Customer.io have far better event-driven email capabilities
Developer-first teams — Mailchimp and Klaviyo have better developer tooling; Constant Contact targets non-technical users
Large enterprise marketing — HubSpot Marketing Hub or Marketo are more appropriate for enterprise-scale workflows
best_when: You're integrating with existing small business workflows that already use Constant Contact and need to automate contact import and basic campaign management.
avoid_when: You're building a new email marketing automation system from scratch — Mailchimp or Klaviyo have better APIs and broader adoption.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 required — no API key option. Authorization code flow for user access. Client credentials not supported. Access tokens expire — refresh token rotation required. Scopes: contact_data, campaign_data, account_read, account_update.

pricing.paid_tiers: Lite: $12/month; Standard: $35/month; Premium: $80/month (prices vary by contact count)
pricing.notes: 60-day free trial. Pricing scales with contact count. API access requires at least Core plan. No pay-per-email option — subscription only.

performance.rate_limits: 4 requests/second per access token; contact import async for large lists; campaign operations rate-limited

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API v3 documentation improved from v2 but still less comprehensive than Mailchimp. Error responses include error_key and error_message. OAuth flow is mandatory and adds complexity. Rate limit errors return 429.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Contact upsert by email is idempotent. Campaign creation is not idempotent — duplicate campaigns created without deduplication check. Email sends are irreversible once scheduled.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth 2.0 is mandatory — no API key option means agents need full OAuth implementation before any API calls
Access tokens expire — agents must implement refresh token rotation to maintain long-running operations
Rate limit (4 req/s) requires careful pacing for bulk contact operations
Contact list IDs required for all list operations — pre-fetch list IDs during agent initialization
V2 API (deprecated) and V3 API coexist — documentation sometimes references v2 endpoints; always use v3

security_score_components.security_notes: GDPR and CASL compliant. TLS enforced. OAuth 2.0 required — no static API key risk. Scopes limit access to needed data. US data processing. No SOC2 documented. 60+ years of business email — mature legal compliance.
